title: jacobaea othonnae subsp. othonnae
intro: Jacobaea othonnae subsp. othonnae, also known as Jacob's ladder, is a plant species in the sunflower family that is native to the Iberian Peninsula and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Jacob's ladder include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and support the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Jacob's ladder can be prepared in various herbal forms, including tea, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
